courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Environmental & Urban Economics
courses.description: This course will study urban economic issues associated with environmental and social issues within urban regions. The course will study this topic from an interdisciplinary perspective. A range of topics in urban economics will be discussed including planning policies related to urban environment, migration, and transportation, as well as, zoning and liveability issues, and potentials for more sustainable, just and liveable cities. Students will become familiar with contemporary urban economic research and online sources of economic data. The course will prepare students to develop criteria for value judgments about the complex social, economic and environmental impact of urban design and planning.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MSc in Urban Design, MSc in Advanced Environmental Planning Technologies, and MSc in Landscape Architecture
courses.learning_outcomes:
On successful completion of this unit, students will be able to:
1. Demonstrate a basic understanding of the principles of macro and micro economic theory and the valuation of environmental resources.
2. Apply elementary methods of economic analysis to contemporary issues relating to the planning and management of built and natural environments.
courses.course_syllabus: This course studies urban economic issues associated with environmental and social issues within urban regions. The course will study this topic from an interdisciplinary perspective. A range of topics in urban economics will be discussed including planning policies related to urban environment, migration, and transportation, as well as, zoning and liability issues, and potentials for more sustainable, just and liveable cities. Students will become familiar with contemporary urban economic research and online sources of economic data. The course will prepare students to develop criteria for value judgments about the complex social, economic and environmental impact of urban design and planning.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Course evaluations, feedback to essays, external examiner report .
courses.required_readings: Please refer to the recommended readings.

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Urban History & Theory
courses.description: This course examines the main ideas, histories and theories of the city through specific local and international examples from significant historical, political, geographical and environmental periods and aspects. Important themes and debates in urban design and planning will be critically explored to understand the relationship between the urban forms, the culture of cities and their sustainable development. In particular, the way urban forms have been used and reinterpreted, adapted and challenged by different social, economic and political contexts. Exemplary design projects and planning policies are investigated in view of their relationship to contexts and their impacts on communities and the environment.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MSc in Urban Design and Master of Architecture;Mutually exclusive to ARCH5721
courses.learning_outcomes:
The course aims to familiarize students with the histories and theories of urbanism and the role of design in shaping city forms and social environments. It seeks to equip students with knowledge of city planning, design, and development policy, as well as the needs and aspirations of local communities. Through in-class discussions and debates, students are encouraged to challenge their preconceived assumptions and analyse various urban issues from comparative perspectives. The learning process will also help them develop critical thinking and academic writing skills relevant to research in architecture and urban studies.

Students are allowed to use AI tools in some learning activities as specified in the individual assignment handouts. In such cases, they are required to explicitly acknowledge the use of these tools, and must document the specific tools used, how they were employed, their assessment of the feedback received from the tools and whether the feedback was incorporated into their submitted work.
courses.course_syllabus:
The course familiarizes students with various methods for studying cities as pursued in the social sciences and humanities, including history, geography, and urban studies. Through in-depth discussion and debates of selected case studies, students will gain a historical perspective of urbanization and develop the ability to analyze specific urban issues, identifying continuities and congruences in city development across different time periods and locations.

This course aligns with the framework of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the UN Habitat’s Urban Agenda, emphasizing the importance of interdisciplinary thinking to address today’s pressing urban challenges. A major assignment will require students to explore the concepts of the SDGs and establish meaningful connections with contemporary design practice in Hong Kong.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Course evaluations, individual feedback on papers, external examiner report
courses.required_readings: Please refer to the recommended readings.
courses.recommended_readings: Please refer to the course outline.

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Research Methods in Urban Design

meetings.location: Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G04
Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G04

courses.description: This course consolidates students understanding of academic inquiry and enables them to conduct a range of research methods relevant for the field of urban design. The first part of the course covers general aspects such as research ethics, scoping of research areas, conceptualizing research frameworks and identifying relevant research questions. The second part provides students with the relevant research methods and skills for urban design, such as conducting field studies, surveys and semi-structured interviews, as well as performing a range of qualitative and quantitative analyses. Throughout the course, students will learn how to relate their findings from their mixed-method studies to address complex urban issues. Assignments are organized according to sub-themes linked to the core issues of urban design.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MSc in Urban Design, MArch, and MSc in Landscape Architecture
courses.learning_outcomes: At the completion of this course, students will be able (1) to understand core aspects of academic research (including research ethics, development of research frameworks etc.), (2) to study and apply a range of research methods for the investigation of complex urban contexts and sites, and (3) to integrate their findings into their urban design processes. Thus, the course will prepare students for their final thesis project as well as foster their ability of life-long learning.
courses.course_syllabus: Please refer to to course outline.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Course evaluations, individual feedback on papers, external examiner report

Eco, Umberto (author), Francesco Erspamer (introduction). How to Write a Thesis. Cambridge, MA: MIT Press, 2015.

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Urban Design Studio I

meetings.instructor:
Ms. CHEUNG Tin Yan, 
Professor Jeroen Bastiaan VAN AMEIJDE

Ms. CHEUNG Tin Yan, 
Professor Jeroen Bastiaan VAN AMEIJDE

Ms. CHEUNG Tin Yan, 
Professor Jeroen Bastiaan VAN AMEIJDE

Ms. CHEUNG Tin Yan, 
Professor Jeroen Bastiaan VAN AMEIJDE

courses.description: Studio 1 focuses on field work, community engagement, participatory design, and strategic urban development approaches for an existing area in Hong Kong. Through engagement with key stakeholders from the community, private sector and local authorities, the studio will critically analyse existing development plans and directions, balancing economic objectives with the need for human-centric, community-oriented development. Student work in multi-disciplinary teams on research and design tasks, including stakeholder engagement, urban morphology mapping studies, and networks/flows analysis, leading towards a neighbourhood and placemaking-oriented urban design proposal.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MSc in Urban Design and Master of Architecture.
courses.learning_outcomes: Students begin to understand key urban issues, to come up with innovative and creative place making solutions to address some of the urban issues. Students develop capability to analyse urban forms and the forces resulting in the transformation of the urban fabric in existing areas and neighbourhoods. Students learn to facilitate participation of multiple stakeholders in a workshop setting and to help develop proposals and urban design strategies resuting design intervention at the local in community scale. Students gain experience working in groups and individually, to be better prepared for the real world, fostering critical thinking skills, about how to address community and environmental issues in a context sensitive and sustainable way.
courses.course_syllabus: UD Studio I focuses on existing urban districts in the Greater Bay Area including Hong Kong and Macau and beyond. Students develop in groups and as individuals a design project exploring opportunities for neighborhoods and public spaces.
courses.feedback_evaluation:
Course Evaluation
Weekily feedback in desk critiques for indiviaual students and groups.
Sequence of reviews with invited critics from academia and practice.
External examiners after final review.

courses.title: Urban Design Studio II

courses.description: Urban Design Studio II explores theories and methodologies for human-centric urban development and regeneration. Via historic, site and user research, students will first obtain a clear understanding of the neighbourhood’s physical characteristics, identity, problems and pain points, and social processes. Precedent studies from around the world will equip them with tools of engagement, intervention, renewal or development, to formulate their own methodologies in reviving an existing site or neighbourhood in Hong Kong or the Greater Bay Area. Students work in multi-disciplinary teams on critical analysis, data mapping, user research and validation, and application of current and proposed planning tools to develop a comprehensive and sustainable urban design proposal that balances bottom-up and top-down mechanisms.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
For students of MSc in Urban Design and Master of Architecture.
Prerequiste: URBD5710 or ARCH5111
courses.learning_outcomes: 1) Development of advanced design skills in urban design; 2) Capability to analyse urban and land forms and their interrelation; 3) Development of design proposals based on an integration of knowledge of complex ecosystems; 4) Integration of different urban transport networks based on their capability to create a liveable and sustainable environment; 5) Development of the ability to work in teams and with various professional consultants; 6) Fostering critical thinking.
courses.course_syllabus: Urban Design Studio II focuses on the creation of a new urban area. Students develop in groups and as individuals on a design project.
courses.feedback_evaluation: 1) Course evaluations; 2) weekly feedback in desk critiques for individual students and groups; 3) sequence of reviews with invited critics from academia and practice; and 4) external examiner reports

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Topical Studies in Urban Design 1 - Urban Processes

meetings.location: Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G01

courses.description: This course focuses on the urban processes involved in the development and redevelopment of cities. It offers an introduction to urban elements and processes including typology, city forms, spatial data, urban regeneration, placemaking, and the role of community engagement in the development of Hong Kong and beyond. The course also focuses on the integration of digital tools in urban design processes, including technologies such as GIS and data mapping to analyse urban spaces. Through lectures, field work assignments, and community workshops, students gain hands on experience by directly engaging with various stakeholders as well as community members. Students will learn from industry experts who will provide practical insights and networking opportunities.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MArch, MSc in Urban Design, MSSc in Housing Studies and MSc in Landscape Architecture
courses.course_syllabus: The course focuses on the urban processes involved in the development and redevelopment of cities. It offers an introduction to urban elements and processes including typology, city forms, spatial data, urban regeneration, placemaking, and the role of community engagement in the development of Hong Kong and beyond. Through lectures, field work assignments, and community workshops, students gain hands on experience by directly engaging with various stakeholders including community members and industry experts.

External examiners

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Topical Studies in Urban Design 2 - Urban Transport Networks
courses.description: The course provides an introduction into history and application of urban transportation systems and their impact on contemporary mobility patterns. Urban integration, policy, transport concepts, planning aspects, technical requirements and ways of implementation along precedents are presented. During the course visiting lectures will share first hand professional knowledge whilst its adaption to student design projects will be encouraged.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MArch, MSc in Urban Design and MSc in Advanced Environmental Planning Technologies.
courses.learning_outcomes:
Holistic understanding of key infrastructure aspects required for urban design enabling participation in real projects.
Understanding of involvement of stakeholders and various professional disciplines and their roles throughout planning process.
Knowledge of means of communication to various stakeholders realistically participating in similar projects.
courses.course_syllabus: The course provides an introduction into history and application of complex urban transportation systems that are globally in use or conception. It will provide introduction to transport concepts, planning aspects, technical requirements and ways of implementation.
courses.feedback_evaluation:
Course evaluations
Feedback on student papers
External examiners

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Topical Studies in Urban Design 4 - International Workshop

meetings.instructor:
Ms. CHEUNG Tin Yan, 
Professor LAU Sing Yeung Sunnie

Ms. CHEUNG Tin Yan, 
Professor LAU Sing Yeung Sunnie

Ms. CHEUNG Tin Yan, 
Professor LAU Sing Yeung Sunnie

courses.description: This course is organized as an intensive workshop and allows students to explore in teams with students and advisors from various international universities a specific issue of urban design. Different international urban conditions are explored through comparative analyses and design. Students can benefit from the exposure to different urban cultures and design approaches. The course also prepares students for a working practice in diverse teams and in different cultural contexts.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MSc in Urban Design and MSc in Landscape Architecture
courses.learning_outcomes: In an increasingly globalized design practice, the international workshop offers the opportunity to: 1) increase the understanding of different urban conditions and cultures; 2) to learn from different design approaches; 3) to train the working in interdisciplinary and international teams; 4) and, to formulate coherent design and planning concepts in a short period of time.
courses.course_syllabus: This course is organized as an intensive workshop and allows students to explore in teams with students and advisors from various international universities a specific issue of urban design. Different international urban conditions are explored through comparative analyses and design. Students can benefit from the exposure to different urban cultures and design approaches. The course also prepares students for a working practice in diverse teams and in different cultural contexts.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Course evaluations, sequence of reviews with invited critics from academia and practice, external examiner report.
courses.required_readings: Please refer to the recommended readings.
courses.recommended_readings: Literature will be selected according to the specific theme and place of the international workshop.

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Environmental Design for Liveable and Healthy Cities

meetings.location: Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G01
Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G01
Lee Shau Kee Archi Bldg G01

courses.description: This course aims at providing a comprehensive and multidisciplinary understanding of sustainable and environmental design, by gathering and connecting a wide range of contemporary perspectives from academia and practice. Organised as a lecture series by a range of disciplinary experts, the course exposes students to the latest research, project examples and policy initiatives aimed at improving the economic, social and environmental sustainability of urban environments.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students of MArch, MSc in Urban Design and MSc in Landscape Architecture
courses.course_syllabus: Please refer to the course outline.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Course evaluations, individual feedback on papers, external examiner report
courses.required_readings: Please refer to the course outline.
courses.recommended_readings: Please refer to the course outline.

courses.subject: URBD
courses.title: Urban Design Thesis

meetings.instructor:
Professor CHAN Man Ha, 
Professor Jeroen Bastiaan VAN AMEIJDE

Professor CHAN Man Ha, 
Professor Jeroen Bastiaan VAN AMEIJDE

courses.description: This course covers the general procedures of theoretical inquiry as it relates to urban design, from scoping the research terrain, identifying and focusing on conceptual frameworks and research questions, to determining the techniques, scope and phasing of investigations, as well as criteria for the development of interpretations and paradigms. For the dissertation, students will develop a proposal and produce intellectually rigorous piece of written or designed work. The topic will be defined in discussion with advisors, either in relationship with the design studio, or as an independent research on a topic related to urban design.
courses.enrollment_requirement:
For students of MSc in Urban Design.
Prerequisite: URBD5720 Urban Design Studio II
courses.learning_outcomes:
The main objective of the thesis work is to provide an opportunity for the student to conduct an independent research and/or design proposal related to a specific interest in the field of Urban Design. The student shall learn to formulate research and/or design problem, define researchable questions and develop solutions for the defined problems.

Students will implement their knowledge on an advanced level and demonstrate an in-depth capacity to address challenges stemming from the complex ways in which cities function, grow and develop. The students will gain the professional and theoretical tools to conduct an in-depth research as well as to implement their skills and knowledge through the process of design or a written report.
courses.course_syllabus: This course covers the general procedures of theoretical inquiry as it relates to urban design, from scoping the research terrain, identifying and focusing on conceptual frameworks and research questions, to determining the techniques, scope and phasing of investigations, as well as criteria for the development of interpretations and paradigms. For the dissertation, students will develop a proposal and produce intellectually rigorous piece of written or designed work. The topic will be defined in discussion with advisors, either in relationship with the design studio, or as an independent research on a topic related to urban design.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Course evaluation, sequence of reviews, external examiner report.
courses.required_readings: Please refer to the recommended readings.
courses.recommended_readings: Literature will depend on individual topics.
